scope: Pass B full-surface expansion: every endpoint documented under the public Coda API v1 reference (docs/folders/pages/tables/rows/columns/formulas/controls/permissions/publishing/analytics/misc). Coda's separate admin/v1 and Packs-maker APIs are a distinct product surface (workspace-admin and Pack-publisher tooling, not doc-data access) and are out of scope for this source/destination connector.

excluded.reason: creates a brand-new doc (optionally copying an existing one); doc-lifecycle management is a workspace-admin action outside this connector's per-doc data-sync scope, and duplicating an arbitrary sourceDoc has no bounded blast radius to express as a plain row/page mutation
single-doc detail is a strict subset of the docs stream's per-record shape already returned by GET /docs; no additional fields worth a dedicated stream
irreversibly deletes an entire doc and everything in it; far outside the bounded per-row/per-page mutation surface this connector exposes
renames/re-icons the doc itself; doc-level metadata management, not doc-scoped data sync
sharing/ACL metadata is workspace-permission surface, not doc content data
lists who has access to the doc; permission/sharing management is out of scope for a content connector
grants doc access to a principal; access-control mutation, not content data
revokes doc access; access-control mutation, not content data
searches workspace principals for sharing purposes; identity/directory surface, not doc content
doc-level sharing default settings; access-control configuration, not content data
mutates doc-level sharing defaults; access-control configuration, not content data
single-control detail is a strict subset of the controls stream's per-record shape already returned by GET /docs/{docId}/controls
lists custom domains bound to the doc; workspace/domain-administration surface, not doc content data
binds a custom domain to the doc; domain-administration mutation, not content data
unbinds a custom domain; domain-administration mutation, not content data
updates a custom domain binding; domain-administration mutation, not content data
single-formula detail is a strict subset of the formulas stream's per-record shape already returned by GET /docs/{docId}/formulas

triggers a doc-authored automation rule by webhook; the rule's own logic (which may itself write anywhere in the doc) is opaque to this connector and has no bounded, declarable request/response shape beyond a bare trigger POST with no standard body contract
single-page detail is a strict subset of the pages stream's per-record shape already returned by GET /docs/{docId}/pages
begins an async export of page content to HTML/Markdown; the result is a downloadable blob fetched via a follow-up polling request, not a declarative JSON record read or write
polls the status/download link of an in-flight content export job (see the export POST above); job-status polling, not a data resource
publishes the doc (or changes its publish settings) to a public/discoverable URL; a workspace-publishing action, not per-record content data
unpublishes the doc; a workspace-publishing action, not per-record content data
single-table detail is a strict subset of the tables stream's per-record shape already returned by GET /docs/{docId}/tables
single-column detail is a strict subset of the columns stream's per-record shape already returned by GET /docs/{docId}/tables/{tableIdOrName}/columns
single-row detail is a strict subset of the rows stream's per-record shape already returned by GET /docs/{docId}/tables/{tableIdOrName}/rows
lists Coda doc-gallery publish categories (e.g. "Product", "Sales"); a fixed lookup enum for the publishing UI, not workspace/doc content data

polls the completion status of an async mutation request id (rows/pages writes here return 202 immediately); status polling, not a data resource, and this connector's write actions do not themselves poll for completion (matching every other async-202 write action bundle in this migration)
resolves a coda.io browser URL to its API resource id/type; a URL-resolution utility, not a data resource
returns the authenticated user's own identity/token metadata; auth introspection, not workspace/doc content
